behaviors.description: The traveling-salesman heuristic produces a route that is no more than 1.5x the length of the optimal route for inputs up to 20 stops.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the local heuristic algorithm quality bound. No module handoff, OS, network, subprocess, or model output is required to expose this defect; a pure in-memory comparator against an optimal solver catches it.
behaviors.test_description: Run the heuristic on many deterministic stop sets (sizes 3-20), compute optimal route length with an exact solver for each set, and assert heuristic_length/optimal_length <= 1.5.

behaviors.description: The delivery time window estimates are computed correctly from the distance between consecutive stops and the configured average speed for the road type.
behaviors.justification: This is a deterministic formula/mapping rule (time = distance/speed by road type). The failure is in local computation logic, not infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: For a table of distances and road-type speed configs, assert per-leg and total window estimates match expected values (including rounding/units rules).

behaviors.description: The shift-hours validator rejects a route whose total estimated time exceeds the driver's remaining shift hours, returning the excess amount.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is a local validation rule with arithmetic output (excess time). No cross-module wiring is needed to trigger the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Feed estimated route times and remaining shift hours; assert rejection when route_time > remaining_shift and assert exact excess value returned.

behaviors.description: When one address in the list fails geocoding (API returns no results), the service skips that stop and optimizes the remaining addresses, reporting which addresses were skipped.
behaviors.justification: Real failure appears at module boundaries: geocoding result handling -> filtered stop set -> optimizer input -> skipped-address reporting. In-process fakes are sufficient; real HTTP is not required for this behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Wire service + geocode client adapter (stubbed) + optimizer module. Return no-results for one address and valid coords for others; assert failed address is reported as skipped and route is generated only from successful addresses.

behaviors.description: Duplicate addresses in the input list are consolidated into a single stop with combined delivery items, not visited twice.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily a state handoff/orchestration defect risk: dedup+merge output must propagate to downstream routing so the stop is visited once. A single-function unit test can miss cross-module wiring regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Execute service path with duplicate addresses carrying distinct items; assert output contains one consolidated stop with merged items and final route includes that stop once.
